images.caption: Figure 1:Overview of Foresight.Foresight consists of three stages.Stage 1:we fine-tune an action-conditioned world model (WM-AC) on robot rollouts consisting of image observationsI1:TI_{1:T}and actionsa1:T−1a_{1:T-1}.Stage 2:for each timesteptt, the world model encodes the current observation context into hidden latentszthz_{t}^{h}and predicts action-conditioned future latentsztpz_{t}^{p}using the policy-predicted action chunkAtA_{t}. These latent tokens, together with positional encodings, are passed into a causal sequence model to produce per-timestep failure scoressts_{t}.Stage 3:a conformal calibration set is used to construct a time-varying thresholdδt\delta_{t}(orange line), and a rollout is flagged as failure once the failure score (blue line) is higher than the thresholdst≥δts_{t}\geq\delta_{t}.
Figure 2:Real-Robot Setup.Left:real-world robot setting for three table-top manipulation tasks using ReactorX-200 arm.Right:real-world robot setting for a three-toy picking task using Franka arm.

Figure 8:LIBERO-Long (True Negative)(α=0.02\alpha{=}0.02, Task 0).“Put both the alphabet soup and the tomato sauce in the basket.”The failure scorests_{t}(blue) remains below the FCP thresholdδt\delta_{t}(red dashed)
throughout all inference steps; no alarm is raised and all frame borders are green.

Figure 9:LIBERO-Long (True Positive)(α=0.02\alpha{=}0.02, Task 5).“Pick up the book and place it in the back compartment of the caddy.”Foresight raises an alarm before episode termination as the
action-conditioned world model’s predicted states increasingly diverge from observed states.
The robot failed the task because it dropped the book during the middle of execution.

Figure 10:ManiSkill-Long (True Negative)(α=0.02\alpha{=}0.02, Task 2:Cubes into Bowl).“Put three cubes into the
bowl.”

Figure 11:ManiSkill-Long (True Positive)(α=0.02\alpha{=}0.02, Task 3:Stack 3 Cubes).“Stack 3 cubes together, starting with the red cube.”The robot failed to stack the red cube on the blue cube, leading to the final failure.

Figure 12:BEHAVIOR-1K (True Negative)(α=0.20\alpha{=}0.20, Task 3:Setting Mousetraps).“Take four mousetraps from the bathroom cabinet and place at least two next to the
same sink.”

Figure 13:BEHAVIOR-1K (True Positive)(α=0.20\alpha{=}0.20, Task 47:Cook Hot Dogs).“Take two hot dogs from the refrigerator and cook them in the microwave.”The robot fails during this task because it did not grasp the first hot dog.

Figure 14:Real-world (ReactorX / ACT) (True Negative)(α=0.10\alpha{=}0.10, Pick Banana and toy lion task).“Pick up banana and lion toy into basket.”No false alarm is raised, showing Foresight does not penalize successful executions.

Figure 15:Real-world (ReactorX / ACT) (True Positive)(α=0.10\alpha{=}0.10, Pick Banana and toy lion task).“Pick up banana and lion toy into basket.”A failing real-robot episode from the same task.
The robot failed to pick up the banana, leading to final task failure.
